mechanisms.name: black_ice_formation
mechanisms.description: Black ice forms when temperatures drop below freezing (≤0°C) on wet surfaces
mechanisms.physical_law: Water freezes at 0°C at standard pressure

mechanisms.name: hydroplaning
mechanisms.description: Hydroplaning occurs when water depth exceeds tire tread depth, lifting tire from pavement
mechanisms.physical_law: Hydrodynamic pressure lifts tire when water can't be displaced

mechanisms.name: crosswind_rollover
mechanisms.description: High-sided vehicles can overturn when crosswind force exceeds stability threshold
mechanisms.physical_law: Overturning moment = wind pressure × side area × height

mechanisms.name: fog_reduced_visibility
mechanisms.description: Dense fog reduces visibility, increasing stopping distance and reaction time
mechanisms.physical_law: Stopping distance = reaction distance + braking distance

mechanisms.name: wet_road_reduced_friction
mechanisms.description: Wet roads reduce tire friction, increasing stopping distance by 30-50%
mechanisms.physical_law: Friction coefficient decreases by 30-50% on wet pavement

mechanisms.name: brake_fade
mechanisms.description: Prolonged braking on descent causes brake overheating and reduced stopping power
mechanisms.physical_law: Brake friction coefficient decreases exponentially with temperature

mechanisms.name: tire_blowout_asymmetric_drag
mechanisms.description: Sudden tire deflation creates asymmetric drag, pulling vehicle toward failed tire
mechanisms.physical_law: Rolling resistance increases dramatically on failed tire

mechanisms.name: dooring
mechanisms.description: Opening car door into cyclist's path causes collision with minimal reaction time
mechanisms.physical_law: Cyclist reaction time at 20 km/h is <1 second

mechanisms.name: cargo_shift_destabilization
mechanisms.description: Improperly secured cargo shifts during maneuvers, altering the vehicle's center of gravity and causing loss of control or rollover.
mechanisms.physical_law: Conservation of momentum; Torque = Force x Distance from pivot

mechanisms.name: driver_fatigue_reaction_delay
mechanisms.description: Prolonged driving hours lead to microsleep or delayed cognitive processing, increasing reaction time for urban hazards.
mechanisms.physical_law: Human reaction time increases linearly with sleep deprivation hours

mechanisms.name: blind_spot_encroachment
mechanisms.description: Large logistics vehicles have significant 'no-zones' where smaller road users (cyclists/pedestrians) are invisible to the driver.
mechanisms.physical_law: Angle of incidence vs field of view limits

mechanisms.name: infrastructure_overload_strike
mechanisms.description: Logistics vehicles exceeding height or weight limits cause structural damage to urban infrastructure like bridges or tunnels.
mechanisms.physical_law: Normal force exceeds material yield strength upon impact

mechanisms.name: double_parking_congestion_trigger
mechanisms.description: Delivery vehicles parking in active lanes force traffic into unexpected maneuvers, creating 'near-miss' scenarios or rear-end collisions.
mechanisms.physical_law: Fluid dynamics of traffic flow: capacity drops as lanes decrease

mechanisms.name: thermal_expansion_buckling
mechanisms.description: High ambient temperatures cause road surface materials (especially rail or older asphalt) to expand and buckle, creating sudden vertical hazards.
mechanisms.physical_law: Linear thermal expansion: ΔL = αL₀ΔT

mechanisms.name: crowd_turbulence_bottleneck
mechanisms.description: During public events, high-density crowds behave like a fluid; sudden movements create 'shockwaves' that push pedestrians into the path of logistics vehicles.
mechanisms.physical_law: Lighthill-Whitham-Richards (LWR) model for flow density

mechanisms.name: unmarked_construction_excavation
mechanisms.description: Road maintenance involving open trenches or milled surfaces without proper transition wedges causes suspension failure or tire snagging.
mechanisms.physical_law: Impact force = (mass × velocity²) / stopping distance

mechanisms.name: sun_glare_disability
mechanisms.description: Low-angle sun (dawn/dusk) creates intense specular reflection off wet roads or glass buildings, causing temporary functional blindness.
mechanisms.physical_law: Lambert’s Cosine Law for luminous intensity

mechanisms.name: loose_chippings_traction_loss
mechanisms.description: New 'surface dressing' maintenance leaves loose gravel; high speeds cause these chippings to act like ball bearings, reducing friction.
mechanisms.physical_law: Rolling resistance of granular media

mechanisms.name: bridge_strike_geometry
mechanisms.description: Logistics vehicles exceeding height clearances suffer structural failure upon impact with overhead infrastructure.
mechanisms.physical_law: Inelastic collision: Rigid body geometry exceeds spatial constraints

mechanisms.name: liquid_cargo_slosh
mechanisms.description: Half-full liquid tankers experience dynamic center-of-gravity shifts during cornering, leading to unpredictable lateral forces.
mechanisms.physical_law: Fluid dynamics: Free surface effect shifting the center of gravity

mechanisms.name: milled_surface_tramlining
mechanisms.description: Road maintenance involving longitudinal grooves (milling) forces tires to follow the groove path, causing 'tramlining' and loss of steering control.
mechanisms.physical_law: Lateral force generation via tire-tread and groove interlocking

mechanisms.name: thermal_expansion_buckling
mechanisms.description: Extreme heat causes road materials to expand beyond joint capacity, resulting in vertical heaving or 'blowouts'.
mechanisms.physical_law: Linear thermal expansion: ΔL = αL₀ΔT

mechanisms.name: crowd_shockwave_turbulence
mechanisms.description: High pedestrian density at events creates shockwaves where movement at the front causes a delayed, amplified 'surge' at the back.
mechanisms.physical_law: Fluid-like flow models (LWR) for high-density crowds

mechanisms.name: sun_glare_disability
mechanisms.description: Specular reflection from low-angle sun (dawn/dusk) off wet roads or glass buildings causes temporary functional blindness.
mechanisms.physical_law: Lambert’s Cosine Law for luminous intensity and disability glare

mechanisms.name: viscous_aquaplaning_thin_film
mechanisms.description: A very thin film of water mixed with road oil creates a viscous layer that reduces friction even at low speeds.
mechanisms.physical_law: Boundary lubrication theory: Friction = f(viscosity, speed, pressure)

mechanisms.name: jackknife_instability
mechanisms.description: An articulated vehicle folds because the drive wheels lock or lose traction, causing the trailer to push the cab out of alignment.
mechanisms.physical_law: Vector instability: Momentum of trailer > Frictional force of tractor rear axle

mechanisms.name: venturi_effect_tunnel_gust
mechanisms.description: Air speed increases as it is forced through narrow urban gaps or tunnels, creating sudden lateral forces on vehicles exiting the structure.
mechanisms.physical_law: Bernoulli's Principle: Fluid velocity increases as cross-sectional area decreases

mechanisms.name: ice_bridge_sublimation
mechanisms.description: Bridges freeze before the surrounding roadway because they lack the thermal mass of the earth and are exposed to air on both sides.
mechanisms.physical_law: Newton's Law of Cooling: Increased surface area exposure leads to faster heat loss

mechanisms.name: coning_and_cratering_pothole
mechanisms.description: Freeze-thaw cycles cause pavement to expand and crack; vehicle weight then collapses the void, creating a sudden vertical hazard.
mechanisms.physical_law: Volumetric expansion of water (approx. 9%) upon freezing

mechanisms.name: leaf_mulch_lubrication
mechanisms.description: Wet leaves on the road compress into a waxy, low-friction organic film, creating conditions similar to ice.
mechanisms.physical_law: Boundary lubrication: Pectin and water create a viscous slip layer

mechanisms.name: hydrodynamic_spray_obscuration
mechanisms.description: Large tires aerosolize water on the road, creating a 'rooster tail' spray that reduces visibility for following and passing vehicles.
mechanisms.physical_law: Centrifugal ejection and atomization of fluid by rotating treads

mechanisms.name: pork_chop_maneuver_swing
mechanisms.description: Long vehicles must swing wide left to make a tight right turn, creating a temporary gap that lures cyclists into a 'crush zone'.
mechanisms.physical_law: Off-tracking: Rear wheels follow a shorter path than front wheels

mechanisms.name: whiteout_glare
mechanisms.description: Diffuse light in heavy snow eliminates shadows and horizons, causing loss of spatial orientation and depth perception.
mechanisms.physical_law: Multiple Mie scattering of light by ice crystals

mechanisms.name: vapor_lock_fuel_starvation
mechanisms.description: Fuel in lines turns to gas due to engine heat and high ambient temperature, causing the engine to stall unexpectedly.
mechanisms.physical_law: Phase change: Liquid fuel reaches boiling point at ambient pressure

mechanisms.name: resonance_frequency_wobble
mechanisms.description: Regular road ripples (washboarding) match the vehicle's suspension natural frequency, leading to uncontrolled bouncing and loss of contact.
mechanisms.physical_law: Forced oscillation: Driving frequency = Natural frequency ($f_0$)

mechanisms.name: mirage_depth_distortion
mechanisms.description: Hot air near the road surface refracts light, creating 'puddles' that obscure hazards or cause drivers to brake unnecessarily.
mechanisms.physical_law: Snell's Law: Refraction through air density gradients

mechanisms.name: brake_line_moisture_freeze
mechanisms.description: Accumulated condensation in pneumatic brake lines freezes, preventing brake release or application in heavy logistics vehicles.
mechanisms.physical_law: Freezing point of water; Boyle's Law (condensation under pressure)

mechanisms.name: pavement_bleeding_slick
mechanisms.description: In extreme heat, asphalt binder melts and rises to the surface, creating a 'tacky' then 'slick' layer with very low friction.
mechanisms.physical_law: Viscosity reduction of bitumen as a function of temperature

mechanisms.name: wildlife_collision_braking_chain
mechanisms.description: Sudden appearance of animals causes high-G braking maneuvers, leading to rear-end collisions in high-speed corridors.
mechanisms.physical_law: Kinetic energy dissipation during emergency braking

mechanisms.name: bridge_expansion_joint_gap
mechanisms.description: Cold weather causes bridge sections to contract, widening expansion joints and creating 'tire-trap' gaps for cyclists or small wheels.
mechanisms.physical_law: Thermal contraction: $\Delta L = \alpha L_0 \Delta T$ (negative $\Delta T$)

mechanisms.name: gravel_windrow_instability
mechanisms.description: Grading of unpaved roads leaves a 'windrow' of loose material in the center; crossing this causes sudden asymmetric drag and swerving.
mechanisms.physical_law: Displacement resistance: Drag force $\propto$ depth of loose medium

mechanisms.name: tire_bead_unseating
mechanisms.description: Low tire pressure combined with high lateral G-forces (sharp turns) causes the tire to peel off the rim, resulting in instant loss of control.
mechanisms.physical_law: Internal pressure < Lateral force component at the bead seat

mechanisms.name: shadow_ice_persistence
mechanisms.description: In hilly or forested areas, ice remains in shaded sections of the road long after the rest of the road has thawed.
mechanisms.physical_law: Radiative heat transfer: Lack of shortwave solar radiation prevents melting

mechanisms.name: dust_devil_buffeting
mechanisms.description: Small, intense vortices in dry conditions create sudden, localized pressure drops and erratic wind gusts.
mechanisms.physical_law: Convective instability: Rapid vertical air movement with rotation

mechanisms.name: hydroplaning_rebound_collision
mechanisms.description: After a vehicle regains traction post-hydroplane, the tires are often steered at an angle, causing the vehicle to dart suddenly into an adjacent lane.
mechanisms.physical_law: Static friction > Kinetic friction; sudden lateral force application

mechanisms.name: visual_distraction_mobile_phone
mechanisms.description: Driver looking at mobile phone diverts visual attention from roadway, increasing reaction time by 2-3 seconds
mechanisms.physical_law: Visual reaction time increases from ~0.75s to ~2.5s when eyes off road

mechanisms.name: cognitive_distraction_conversation
mechanisms.description: Engaging in conversation (passenger, hands-free call) diverts cognitive resources, reducing hazard detection
mechanisms.physical_law: Cognitive distraction causes 'inattentional blindness' — missing up to 50% of peripheral hazards

mechanisms.name: manual_distraction_reaching
mechanisms.description: Driver reaching for object (phone, food, navigation) removes hands from steering wheel, reducing control
mechanisms.physical_law: One hand off steering wheel reduces corrective steering capacity by 50-70%

mechanisms.name: visual_distraction_GPS
mechanisms.description: Driver looking at GPS screen diverts eyes from roadway, typical glance duration 1.5-2 seconds
mechanisms.physical_law: At 100 km/h, 2 seconds of eyes-off-road = 55 meters traveled blind

mechanisms.name: visual_distraction_external
mechanisms.description: Driver looking at external distraction (accident, billboard, pedestrian) diverts attention from driving task
mechanisms.physical_law: Rubbernecking reduces effective scanning and increases rear-end collision risk by 40%

mechanisms.name: distraction_combined
mechanisms.description: Multiple simultaneous distractions (e.g., phone + conversation) compound reaction time deficits
mechanisms.physical_law: Combined distractions can increase reaction time by 3-5x compared to baseline
